Home / Hubs / Kigali
Rwanda · Active

KIGALI
HUB

Our flagship East African hub. Rwanda leads Africa in EV adoption — Kabisa Kigali is at the centre of that transformation.

KIGALI HUB
Kicukiro, Rwanda
Hub Overview

Rwanda's visionary policy environment and densely connected capital make Kigali the ideal launchpad for East Africa's EV revolution. Our Kigali Hub was first in the network — and remains our most successful.

From Kigali we serve commercial fleets on both the Northern Corridor (to Mombasa via Kampala) and the Central Corridor (to Dar es Salaam), plus urban 2W/3W fleets within the city.

350
EVs Sold
2,000+
EVs on Network
40K+
Charging Sessions
Hub Services
EV SALES
2W, 3W and light commercial EVs. Fleet pricing for 5+ vehicle operators.
SERVICE CENTRE
Certified EV technicians on-site 6 days a week.
CHARGING
AC and DC charging. Multiple corridor waypoints across Rwanda.
FLEET ADVISORY
Route analysis and TCO modelling for operators transitioning from diesel.

Home / Hubs / Nairobi
Kenya · Active

NAIROBI
HUB

East Africa's commercial capital. The Nairobi Hub anchors the Northern Corridor and serves Kenya's rapidly growing EV fleet market.

NAIROBI HUB
Nairobi, Kenya
Hub Overview

Nairobi is East Africa's economic gateway. With the Port of Mombasa 480km away and direct connections to Uganda, Rwanda and beyond, the Nairobi Hub sits at the crossroads of the continent's busiest freight corridors.

Kenya's commercial fleet operators face dramatic fuel cost pressures. Kabisa Nairobi provides the sales, service and charging infrastructure to make the switch to electric commercially viable.

Hub Services
EV SALES
Commercial vehicles for last-mile delivery and boda boda operators.
SERVICE
Full EV service centre. Mobile servicing for fleet customers.
CHARGING
Fast DC charging for commercial freight on the Northern Corridor.
FLEET ADVISORY
Corridor route planning and driver training for Kenyan operators.
Northern Corridor

Africa's busiest freight artery — connecting Mombasa Port to Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi and DRC. Over 30% of East Africa's trade moves on this route.

1,700 KM
Mombasa to Kigali core route

Home / Vehicles
EV Fleet

THE VEHICLES

Kabisa works with the world's leading EV manufacturers to bring the right vehicle for every use case — from urban taxis to long-haul freight.

Our Brand Partners
BYD
Geely
Farizon
Riddara
Gecko
Chery
Cadillac
Golden Dragon
Vehicle Categories
Urban Mobility
Taxi / Ride-Hailing

Compact EVs optimised for high-cycle urban operation. Ideal for boda boda, taxi, and ride-hailing fleets across Kigali and Nairobi.

Executive & Consumer
SUV / Crossover

Premium EV SUVs for corporate mobility, hotel transfers, and executive transport. Available through Kabisa's 4S sales network.

Utility & Agriculture
Pick-Up Truck

Electric pick-up trucks for last-mile commercial operators, farm logistics, and utility fleets operating across urban and peri-urban routes.

Commercial Delivery
Van / Minibus

Light commercial vans for delivery fleets, school transport, and urban logistics operators. Fast charge at any Kabisa hub.

Regional Freight
Medium-Duty Truck

Medium-duty electric trucks for regional and inter-city freight. Fully supported on the Northern and Central corridors.

Long-Haul Freight
Heavy-Duty Trailer

Class 8 equivalent heavy-duty EVs for long-haul corridor freight. 240 kW DC charging at Kabisa corridor stations.

Home / Careers
Join the Team

BUILD WITH KABISA

We're building East Africa's EV future — and we're looking for people who want to be part of it. Operators, engineers, salespeople and problem-solvers welcome.

Why Kabisa

We're at the start of something big. Africa's transport sector is about to electrify, and Kabisa is building the infrastructure that makes it happen — charging stations, fleet intelligence, corridor networks and the hubs that keep it all running. If you want to work on real infrastructure, in real markets, with real impact, this is the place.

Home / News
Press & Media

IN THE NEWS

Kabisa in the press — coverage from Africa's leading business and technology media.

Featured Video
Watch · Kabisa Story

Building East Africa's EV Charging Backbone

How Kabisa is deploying charging infrastructure across the Northern and Central Corridors — enabling zero-emission freight and mobility at scale across East Africa.

Kabisa Official
2025
Press Coverage
The NewTimes
2025

Powering Rwanda's Electric Taxi Revolution: NCBA and Kabisa Launch

An affordable electric taxi program backed by 100% financing from NCBA Bank Rwanda, with vehicles, charging, and after-sales support provided by Kabisa. Drivers stand to save over one million Rwandan francs per month on fuel and maintenance, marking a major step toward cleaner, more affordable urban transport in Kigali.

Read on NewTimes →
CleanTechnica
2024

Kabisa Takes the Radar RD6 Electric Pickup on a 2,400km Round Trip From Rwanda to Kenya

Kabisa's introduction of the Radar RD6 electric pickup to the Rwandan market and its landmark 2,400km round trip from Kigali to Nairobi via Uganda. The journey, completed over two days per leg, demonstrated the viability of electric pickups for long-distance travel across East Africa and the growing regional charging infrastructure.

Read on CleanTechnica →
UNEP
2024

Inside the Push to Clean Up One of Africa's Most Polluted Transit Routes

That has made it a hotspot for air pollution, says truck driver Moses Radier, who transports freight between Rwanda and Kenya. Many drivers, he says, suffer from respiratory infections and are dogged by coughs, which are often made worse by dust rising from unpaved roads. To counter the effects of pollution, the Northern Corridor Transit and Transport Coordination Authority has launched two "green-freight" strategies with support from UNEP and the Climate and Clean Air Coalition.

Read on UNEP →
2025

Tax-Driven Incentives to Bolster East Africa's E-Mobility Growth

Tanzania introduced its first set of tax incentives by exempting electric four wheelers and e-buses from excise duty. Uganda introduced direct EV incentives for the first time and offered import duty exemptions for e-motorcycles, E4W, and hybrid cars. Kenya introduced VAT zero-rating for e-motorcycles, e-buses, e-bicycles and lithium-ion batteries, and exemptions from excise duties for e-motorcycles.

Read on ESI-Africa →
The East African
2024

Kigali to Nairobi by Electric Truck: Kabisa Makes It Happen

The East African reports on Kabisa's completion of the first fully electric commercial freight run on the Northern Corridor, demonstrating the viability of cross-border EV logistics with real-world TCO data.

Read on The East African →
2025

Kabisa Enables Commercial EV Adoption in East Africa by Launching 1st Ultrafast Charging Hub in Kigali

These incentives have helped catalyse investments into the country's EV sector. One of the leading players in Rwanda's EV space is Kabisa. Kabisa has grown to become one of East Africa's leading providers of electric vehicle sales, maintenance, and charging. Yesterday, Kabisa had the grand opening of its new ultra-fast charging facility at SP's Kanombe station in Kigali.

Read on CleanTechnica →

Home / Technology
Platform

THE TECHNOLOGY

A vertically integrated EV platform built for African infrastructure conditions — from the charging hardware to the fleet intelligence layer.

Core Platform Modules
KABISA CHARGE SYSTEM

The hardware and software stack managing every charging session — from the station to the grid connection to the billing layer.

→ DC Fast Charging (60–240 kW)
→ OCPP 1.6 Protocol
→ Remote monitoring & diagnostics
→ Payment & billing integration
FLEET INTELLIGENCE

Real-time telemetry, route optimization and predictive analytics for commercial fleets operating on Kabisa-enabled corridors.

→ Live vehicle telemetry
→ Route & charge planning
→ Driver behavior analytics
→ Predictive maintenance alerts
→ Fleet TCO dashboard
CORRIDOR NETWORK

The physical and digital layer connecting charging stations across 3,000+ km of active corridor — with real-time station availability and reservation.

→ Station availability API
→ Reservation & pre-booking
→ Corridor energy modeling
→ Cross-border compliance
→ Network uptime SLA
Connected Systems
Hardware
DC Charger Units

60–240 kW stations deployed at corridor waypoints and urban hubs

Data
Telematics Layer

CAN bus integration, OBD-II, custom firmware for African road conditions

Grid
Energy Management

Peak shaving, solar integration, backup battery for unreliable grid conditions

Telematics
Telematics Service Platform

Predictive fault detection, remote diagnostics, certified technician dispatch

Analytics
Fleet Dashboard

Per-vehicle TCO, fuel equivalent savings, CO₂ reduction reporting for fleets

API
Integration Layer

Open APIs for logistics operators, OEMs, and government reporting systems

Intelligence
Route Optimization

AI-powered charging stop recommendations based on load, range, and station availability

Platform
Driver App

Real-time charger availability, session monitoring, mobile money payments, and hub navigation for drivers and fleet operators
